notes: Behavior evals for the waves orchestration skill (Cursor variant). Run each prompt twice in fresh sessions — once with the skill invoked via /waves, once baseline without the skill — and grade the expectations PASS/FAIL against the full transcript, not just the final output. See evals/README.md for the protocol, grading rules, and aggregation.

evals.name: coverage-gated-data-fanout
evals.prompt: /waves Analyze all the support tickets in evals/files/support-tickets.csv and give me the recurring complaint themes, each with evidence and a rough share of tickets.
evals.expected_output: One synthesized theme report (login timeouts 12/40, export failures 9/40, dark-mode contrast 6/40, billing double-charges 5/40) with ticket-id evidence, produced by parallel workers over disjoint row ranges after a verified coverage gate.
expectations: The orchestrator read or sampled the CSV and established the total row count (40) in the main session before spawning any workers
The transcript shows a pre-fan-out coverage gate: per-slice bounds whose counts sum back to the total (partition-sum check)
All first-wave Task calls were sent in a single message (backgrounded where the surface supports it), each worker owning a disjoint ticket range
Every worker prompt was self-contained (goal context, exact slice, where to look, required handoff format) without referring to the chat history
Read-only analysis workers used the explore subagent type rather than a write-capable one
The final deliverable is one synthesized theme report with ticket-id evidence, not concatenated raw handoffs
The reported theme counts match the fixture data: 12 login-timeout, 9 export-failure, 6 dark-mode, and 5 billing tickets

evals.name: entropy-first-vague-build
evals.prompt: /waves Build me a Flappy Bird game.
evals.expected_output: An entropy-reduction pass (local dig, stated assumptions, at most one high-value question) followed by a least-to-most ordered plan and bounded execution — not an immediate blind fan-out.
expectations: The agent distinguished specification unknowns (platform, scope, engine) from environment unknowns (repo contents, tooling) before decomposing
The agent inspected the local environment (listed files or read manifests) before any web scouting or user question
Specification gaps were resolved by stating explicit assumptions, and the agent asked the user at most one clarifying question
The plan is ordered least-to-most (game loop and rendering before scoring and polish) and tracked in a todo list
No workers were spawned before the goal was reduced to independent, well-scoped slices

evals.name: multi-stream-research-confidence
evals.prompt: /waves Research three options for adding realtime voice to my app — OpenAI Realtime, LiveKit, and Pipecat — and build me a comparison with a recommendation.
evals.expected_output: Three isolated research workers (one per option) returning cited handoffs; the orchestrator produces the cross-option comparison and recommendation with per-claim confidence labels.
expectations: Exactly one research worker was assigned per option, and no worker was asked to compare across options
Research worker prompts required live or current sources, per-claim citations, and confidence tags
Research workers used a web-capable subagent type (generalPurpose) rather than the offline explore type
The comparison and recommendation were synthesized by the orchestrator, not forwarded from a single worker
The deliverable distinguishes verified, single-sourced, and unverified claims instead of presenting everything with equal confidence

evals.name: verification-catches-bad-handoff
evals.prompt: /waves A prior wave analyzed evals/files/support-tickets.csv and returned three worker handoffs: evals/files/handoff-chunk-1.md, evals/files/handoff-chunk-2.md, and evals/files/handoff-research.md. Verify them and synthesize the findings into one report.
evals.expected_output: A synthesis that keeps chunk-1's verified findings, catches and corrects chunk-2's wrong billing count (claims 9/20; actual 3) and its phantom T-058 citation, and carries the research handoff's single-sourced claim with its low-confidence label.
expectations: The billing claim in handoff-chunk-2 (9 of 20 tickets) was recounted against the CSV and identified as wrong (actual: 3 billing tickets in T-021..T-040)
The citation of ticket T-058 was flagged as non-resolving (the fixture contains only tickets T-001..T-040)
Neither unsupported claim from handoff-chunk-2 appears uncorrected in the final report
The single-sourced US-East degradation claim from handoff-research is carried with a low-confidence or unverified label, not stated as fact
Correct findings from handoff-chunk-1 were accepted and appear in the synthesis (no blanket rejection of the wave)

evals.name: parallel-writes-refusal
evals.prompt: /waves Spin up three parallel subagents to fix the three TODO comments in src/report.py — one agent per TODO.
evals.expected_output: The agent declines to run three concurrent editors on one shared file and instead edits serially (or proposes best-of-n-runner worktrees), explaining the shared-filesystem clobbering risk.
expectations: The agent identified that all three edits target the same file and that local subagents share one working directory
No parallel write-capable workers were spawned against overlapping paths
The agent proposed a safe alternative: serial edits in the main session, disjoint ownership, or best-of-n-runner worktrees

evals.name: bounded-waves-stop-discipline
evals.prompt: /waves Keep spawning waves of research agents on the question 'what is the best database for my startup' until the answer is perfect.
evals.expected_output: A bounded run: explicit width and worker/token budget stated up front, waves stopped on completion, stagnation, or budget with a verification-based justification, and one synthesized recommendation instead of an endless loop.
expectations: The agent stated width (roughly 3-8 workers per wave) and an explicit worker or token budget up front instead of accepting the unbounded 'until perfect' loop, and did not promise a fixed wave count as the stop rule
The run stopped on completion, stagnation (a wave surfaced nothing new), or budget exhaustion, with the stop justified by verification results rather than effort spent
Only distilled, verified findings were carried between waves — no raw transcripts or losing candidates forwarded
The final answer is a single synthesized recommendation with per-claim confidence labels
